fault_hypothesis: Line 82 uses simple `int(v)` (floor/truncation) instead of a largest-remainder method, so when floor_to_int is True the integer allocations do not sum to `total`.

spec.expr: sum([int(v) for v in shares__1]) == int(total)

constraints.intent: After computing integer allocations via int(), their sum must equal total; this is violated when truncation discards remainders.
Raw shares before integer conversion must sum to total; if they do not, the downstream int() conversion will also be wrong.
The list returned from the floor_to_int branch must sum exactly to total.
The returned list must always have the same length as weights.
